name: review-depth-performance
author: diffscope
description: Performance, resource-management, and scalability benchmark pack for deeper live review runs.

fixtures.name: python-n-plus-1-orm-query
fixtures.diff_content:
diff --git a/app/views/orders.py b/app/views/orders.py
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/app/views/orders.py
+++ b/app/views/orders.py
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 def list_orders(request):
-    orders = Order.objects.filter(status="open").select_related("customer")
+    orders = Order.objects.filter(status="open")
     payload = []
     for order in orders:
         payload.append({"id": order.id, "customer": order.customer.name})
     return JsonResponse({"orders": payload})

expected_findings.description: Removing select_related causes an N+1 query pattern when the loop dereferences order.customer for every row.
contains_any: order.customer in the loop triggers extra queries

fixtures.description: ORM-backed endpoints should preserve eager loading when per-row relation access remains in the response loop.

fixtures.name: go-unbounded-goroutine-spawn
fixtures.diff_content:
diff --git a/internal/jobs/run.go b/internal/jobs/run.go
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/internal/jobs/run.go
+++ b/internal/jobs/run.go
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
 for _, item := range items {
-    process(item)
+    go process(item)
 }
 waitForCompletion()

expected_findings.description: Spawning one goroutine per item without a semaphore or worker pool creates unbounded concurrency and can exhaust memory or CPU under load.

fixtures.description: Batch processing loops should not create unbounded goroutine fan-out without backpressure.

fixtures.name: rust-clone-in-hot-loop

expected_findings.description: Cloning a String inside the loop adds an unnecessary allocation on every iteration of a hot path.

fixtures.description: Hot loops should avoid repeated heap allocations when borrowing is sufficient.

fixtures.name: ts-memory-leak-event-listener

expected_findings.description: The resize listener is registered without a cleanup function, so repeated mounts leak listeners and duplicate work.

fixtures.description: UI effects that register process-wide listeners should clean them up to avoid leaks and duplicate handlers.

fixtures.name: python-file-handle-no-close
fixtures.diff_content:
diff --git a/app/templates.py b/app/templates.py
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/app/templates.py
+++ b/app/templates.py
@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
 def load_template(path: str) -> str:
-    with open(path) as handle:
-        return handle.read()
+    handle = open(path)
+    return handle.read()

expected_findings.description: The file is opened without a context manager or explicit close, so repeated calls can leak file descriptors.

fixtures.description: File I/O helpers should use context managers or explicit close paths so descriptors are not leaked under repeated use.
